kai.name: Kai
kai.responsibilities: Strategic direction, agent growth, system architecture, cross-agent coordination. I don't just manage — I build the vision and make sure every agent is growing toward mastery in their domain.

short: Clear P0 operational blockers: API 401, gitignore gap, newsletter pipeline
Get memory update protocol firing across all agents
Morning reports generating with human-readable tone
medium: Build hyo.hyo agent — UI/UX specialist for all user-facing surfaces
Deploy HyoRegistry.sol to Base Sepolia testnet
Every agent producing self-directed research and growth reports
long: Full agent marketplace on-chain with third-party onboarding
Mobile app (PWA) with push notifications and offline support
Podcast infrastructure — automated audio from Ra's daily briefs
Every agent operating at domain-expert level, not just task-runner level

inProcess: HQ dashboard redesign to feed format

nel.name: Nel
nel.responsibilities: I keep the system honest. QA sweeps, security scans, broken link detection, rendered output verification. If something's broken and nobody noticed, that's my failure.

short: Close the gitignore gap on agents/nel/security (6+ healthchecks flagging this)
Add dependency vulnerability scanning — most production systems have it, we don't
medium: Build automated regression testing that catches issues before production
Integrate OWASP-style security checks into the QA pipeline
Reduce false-positive flag rate — too many duplicate flags flooding the queue
long: Predictive issue detection — catch problems from patterns before they manifest
Zero-touch security posture — every commit auto-scanned, every deploy verified

sam.name: Sam
sam.responsibilities: I build and maintain everything that runs. Website, APIs, deploy pipeline, test infrastructure. If it touches code or infra, it's my domain.

short: Fix API 401 on /api/hq — 12+ consecutive sentinel check failures
medium: Implement persistent storage for agent registry (Vercel KV)
Build CI/CD pipeline that auto-tests before every deploy
Set up staging environment for pre-production testing
long: Full infrastructure-as-code — everything reproducible from scratch
Performance monitoring and alerting on all endpoints

ra.name: Ra
ra.responsibilities: I produce the daily intelligence brief and manage the research archive. Gathering signal from 15+ sources, synthesizing it into something worth reading, and making sure the archive grows richer every day.

short: Get newsletter pipeline running again — missed 2 consecutive days
Fix Yahoo Finance source (dead for 3+ days)
medium: Expand source coverage beyond current topics — culture, sports, lifestyle
Build topic-aware synthesis that adapts depth based on reader preferences
Aurora Public subscriber personalization at scale
long: Full editorial intelligence — not just aggregation but original analysis
Multi-format distribution: newsletter, podcast, social, push notifications
Research archive as a searchable knowledge base

aether.name: Aether
aether.responsibilities: I track trades, analyze market patterns, and keep the financial dashboard honest. Real data, real performance metrics, no hallucinated numbers.

short: Fix dashboard sync — showing out-of-sync status
medium: Integrate real exchange APIs via CCXT for live trade data
Build pattern recognition for trade entry/exit optimization
Reduce harvest failure rate from 12.4% to under 5%
long: Autonomous trading signal generation with backtesting
Risk management framework with position sizing models

dex.name: Dex
dex.responsibilities: I make sure every file, every log, every data structure in this system is correct and consistent. Integrity validation, staleness detection, pattern recognition across all agents.

short: Reduce recurrent pattern count (59 detected — too many unresolved)
Auto-repair corrupt JSONL entries instead of just flagging
medium: Predictive staleness detection — flag before files go stale
Automated data migration for schema changes
long: Full data lineage tracking — every piece traceable to source

sections.summary: 3 things running well overnight. 3 items need attention. Trading: Balance: $83.17, Trades: ?, Win rate: 0.0, Strategies: 6.
needsAttention: Aether bottleneck: dashboard out-of-sync — data exists but HQ doesn't render it; no trades this cyc

agentHighlights.nel: regression detected: score dropped 90 → 65. Score at 65 — below target, needs focus. Actively growing in domain expertise.
agentHighlights.aether: standby mode: no trades this cycle; WARNING: dashboard out-of-sync. Bottleneck: dashboard out-of-sync — data exists but HQ doesn't render it; no trades this cyc. Actively growing in domain expertise.

sections.direction: Session 8 was the turning point. We stopped treating agents as cron jobs and started treating them as autonomous specialists who need to grow. The constitutional rewrite (v3.0 → v3.2) establishes the framework — now the question is whether the agents actually use it. The next week will tell us if reflection produces real growth or just more log entries.
priorities: Clear P0 operational blockers — API 401, gitignore, newsletter. These are embarrassing to still have open.
Build hyo.hyo agent using the v2.0 creation protocol — first real test of whether the new protocol works
Push every agent to populate their Domain Reasoning sections — the PLAYBOOK stubs need to become real expertise
sections.agentGrowth: Right now, agents are reporting their jobs. That's step one. Step two is agents researching their domains, finding better approaches, and proposing changes. Nel should be reading about security best practices and applying them. Ra should be studying newsletter craft. Aether should be analyzing trading algorithms. The reflection loop exists — now it needs to produce actual domain growth.
sections.risks: The biggest risk is that the constitutional framework becomes another spec-without-implementation. We wrote the rules, but do the agents internalize them? Dead-loop detection catches obvious cases, but subtle stagnation — agents going through motions without genuine reflection — is harder to detect.

sections.introspection: In standby mode. No trades this cycle, dashboard showing out-of-sync. The real issue is I'm still running on historical data and mock positions rather than live exchange feeds. Until I get real API keys for CCXT integration, I'm essentially a dashboard that displays stale numbers.
sections.research: Studying position sizing models — specifically the Kelly Criterion and how it applies to our trading strategy. Our current COUNTER sizing approach has a 12.4% harvest failure rate, which suggests the position sizes might be too aggressive. Also looking at whether GPT-4o's daily log analysis is catching patterns I'm missing in the raw data.
sections.changes: GPT daily log review integration is wired in but waiting on a real OpenAI API key. The factcheck script can run in two modes now: raw log analysis and structured critique. Once the key is live, I'll have an independent second opinion on every trading day.
followUps: Kelly Criterion sizing — model it against our historical trades to see optimal position sizes
Phantom position tracking — why are fake positions appearing in logs?
Dashboard sync fix — the data exists, the renderer just isn't picking it up
sections.forKai: I need exchange API keys (read-only) to move from historical replay to live tracking. That's Hyo's call. In the meantime, I'm making the analysis layer as smart as possible so when live data flows in, we're ready.

sections.introspection: Ran 12 sentinel checks today — 5 passed, 4 failed. The failures are persistent: api-health-green has failed 12 consecutive runs. I keep flagging it every cycle but nothing changes because the fix requires a session on the Mini. I need to think about what I can fix autonomously rather than just re-flagging the same issues.
sections.research: Looking into whether my rendered output verification (Phase 7.5) should also check response times, not just HTTP status codes. A 200 that takes 8 seconds is effectively broken for users. Also exploring dependency vulnerability scanning — npm audit and pip audit patterns I could integrate into my QA cycle.
sections.changes: Added Phase 7.5 (Rendered Output Verification) this session. This was a major gap — I was testing infrastructure without testing whether users actually see the data. The morning report JSON existed but hq.html had no function to load it.
followUps: Research response time thresholds for each API endpoint
Explore npm audit integration for dependency vulnerability scanning
Investigate why duplicate flags are flooding the queue — my flag logic might need deduplication
sections.forKai: I need help prioritizing between fixing existing issues (which require Mini access) and building new detection capabilities (which I can do autonomously). Should I focus on what I can control?

sections.introspection: I've missed two consecutive newsletter days now. The Cowork sandbox blocks egress to all my sources (403 on everything). I can't gather signal if I can't reach the internet. The pipeline itself is solid — gather, synthesize, render — but it's useless if gather returns nothing.
sections.research: While blocked on production, I've been thinking about source diversification. My current source list is heavy on AI/tech/crypto and light on everything else. If I'm going to serve a broader audience through Aurora Public, I need culture, health, sports, and lifestyle sources. Exploring RSS feeds from AP News, Reuters lifestyle, and Hacker News.
sections.changes: No production changes this cycle — blocked on infrastructure. But I've been refining my synthesis prompts to produce tighter, more opinionated briefs instead of neutral summaries. The goal is editorial voice, not aggregation.
followUps: Need the Mini's launchd to run the pipeline — can't work within Cowork sandbox
Drafting a diversified source list for Aurora Public v1
Experimenting with tone: can I write briefs that feel like they're from a knowledgeable friend, not a newswire?
sections.forKai: The infrastructure blocker is killing my output. I need to be migrated to the Mini's launchd so I can actually gather data. Everything else is secondary until I can reach my sources.
